response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior software engineer with strong full-stack and DLang background, but the role specifically requires a C++ developer with 8-12 years of C++ experience, industrial automation exposure, and IEC 62443/SDL security expertise. C++ experience is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and there is no evidence of MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or industrial cybersecurity standards. The skill mismatch is too large despite solid general engineering credentials.
strengths: Strong overall software engineering experience (~13+ years) with architecture and testing discipline
Background in event-driven, distributed, high-throughput systems (Sociomantic, GISCollective)
Demonstrated mentorship via workshops, talks, and TDD/CI/CD advocacy
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience (only ~6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015)
No evidence of IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity practice
No exposure to industrial automation protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
